name: monitor session
detail: Create a new Ethernet Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) or a Remote Switched Port Analyzer (RSPAN) session configuration for analyzing traffic between ports or add to an existing session configuration.
syntax: monitor session session-number {destination | filter | source} session-range | remote}
noForm: no monitor session {session-number [destination | filter | source] | all | local | range

params.name: session-number
params.description: The session number identified with the SPAN or RSPAN session. The range is 1 to 68. However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, the range is 1 to 66.

params.name: all
params.description: Clears all monitor sessions.

params.name: local
params.description: Clears all local monitor sessions.

params.name: range session-range
params.description: Clears monitor sessions in the specified range.

params.name: remote
params.description: Clears all remote monitor sessions.

usageSummary: You can set a combined maximum of four local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions.  You can have a total of 68 SPAN and RSPAN sessions on a switch or switch stack.  However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, you are limited to a combined maximum of two local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions, and the range is 1 to 66.

name: monitor session destination
detail: Start a new Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) session or Remote SPAN (RSPAN) destination session, to enable ingress traffic on the destination port for a network security device (such as a Cisco IDS Sensor Appliance), and to add or delete interfaces or VLANs to or from an existing SPAN or RSPAN session.
syntax: monitor session session-number destination {interface interface-id [, | -] [encapsulation {replicate | dot1q} ] {ingress [dot1q | untagged] } | {remote} vlan vlan-id {replicate | dot1q} ] {ingress [dot1q | untagged] } | {remote} vlan vlan-id
noForm: no monitor session session-number destination {interface interface-id [, | -] [encapsulation

params.name: session-number
params.description: The session number identified with the SPAN or RSPAN session. The range is 1 to 68. However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, the range is 1 to 66.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: Specifies the destination or source interface for a SPAN or RSPAN session. Valid interfaces are physical ports (including type, stack member, module, and port number). For source interface, port channel is also a valid interface type, and the valid range is 1 to 128.
(Optional) Specifies a series of interfaces or VLANs, or separates a range of interfaces or VLANs from a previous range. Enter a space before and after the comma.
(Optional) Specifies a range of interfaces or VLANs. Enter a space before and after the hyphen.

params.name: encapsulation replicate
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the destination interface replicates the source interface encapsulation method. If not selected, the default is to send packets in native form (untagged). These keywords are valid only for local SPAN. For RSPAN, the RSPAN VLAN ID overwrites the original VLAN ID; therefore, packets are always sent untagged. The encapsulation options are ignored with the no form of the command. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 304 Network Management monitor session destination

params.name: encapsulation dot1q
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the destination interface accepts the source interface incoming packets with IEEE 802.1Q encapsulation. These keywords are valid only for local SPAN. For RSPAN, the RSPAN VLAN ID overwrites the original VLAN ID; therefore, packets are always sent untagged. The encapsulation options are ignored with the no form of the command.

params.name: ingress
params.description: Enables ingress traffic forwarding.

params.name: dot1q
params.description: (Optional) Accepts incoming packets with IEEE 802.1Q encapsulation with the specified VLAN as the default VLAN.

params.name: untagged
params.description: (Optional) Accepts incoming packets with untagged encapsulation with the specified VLAN as the default VLAN.

params.name: isl
params.description: Specifies ingress forwarding using ISL encapsulation.

params.name: remote
params.description: Specifies the remote VLAN for an RSPAN source or destination session. The range is 2 to 1001 and 1006 to 4094. The RSPAN VLAN cannot be VLAN 1 (the default VLAN) or VLAN IDs 1002 to 1005 (reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs).

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: Sets the default VLAN for ingress traffic when used with only the ingress keyword.

usageSummary: You can set a combined maximum of four local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions.  You can have a total of 68 SPAN and RSPAN sessions on a switch or switch stack.  However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, you are limited to a combined maximum of two local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions, and the range is 1 to 66.

name: monitor session filter
detail: Start a new flow-based SPAN (FSPAN) session or flow-based RSPAN (FRSPAN) source or destination session, or to limit (filter) SPAN source traffic to specific VLANs.
noForm: no monitor session session-number filter {vlan vlan-id [, | -] }

params.name: session-number
params.description: The session number identified with the SPAN or RSPAN session. The range is 1 to 68. However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, the range is 1 to 66.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: Specifies a list of VLANs as filters on trunk source ports to limit SPAN source traffic to specific VLANs. The vlan-id range is 1 to 4094.
(Optional) Specifies a series of VLANs, or separates a range of VLANs from a previous range. Enter a space before and after the comma.
(Optional) Specifies a range of VLANs. Enter a space before and after the hyphen.

usageSummary: You can set a combined maximum of four local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions.  You can have a total of 68 SPAN and RSPAN sessions on a switch or switch stack.  However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, you are limited to a combined maximum of two local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions, and the range is 1 to 66.

name: monitor session source
detail: Start a new Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) session or Remote SPAN (RSPAN) source session, or to add or delete interfaces or VLANs to or from an existing SPAN or RSPAN session.
syntax: monitor session session_number source {interface interface-id [, | -] [both | rx | tx] | [remote] vlan vlan-id [, | -] [both | rx | tx]} [remote] vlan vlan-id [, | -] [both | rx | tx]}
noForm: no monitor session session_number source {interface interface-id [, | -] [both | rx | tx] |

params.name: session_number
params.description: The session number identified with the SPAN or RSPAN session. The range is 1 to 68. However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, the range is 1 to 66.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: Specifies the source interface for a SPAN or RSPAN session. Valid interfaces are physical ports (including type, stack member, module, and port number). For source interface, port channel is also a valid interface type, and the valid range is 1 to 48.
(Optional) Specifies a series of interfaces or VLANs, or separates a range of interfaces or VLANs from a previous range. Enter a space before and after the comma.

(Optional) Specifies a range of interfaces or VLANs. Enter a space before and after the hyphen. both | rx | tx (Optional) Specifies the traffic direction to monitor. If you do not specify a traffic direction, the source interface sends both transmitted and received traffic.

params.name: remote
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the remote VLAN for an RSPAN source or destination session. The range is 2 to 1001 and 1006 to 4094. The RSPAN VLAN cannot be VLAN 1 (the default VLAN) or VLAN IDs 1002 to 1005 (reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs).

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: When used with only the ingress keyword, sets default VLAN for ingress traffic.

usageSummary: Traffic that enters or leaves source ports or source VLANs can be monitored by using SPAN or RSPAN.  Traffic routed to source ports or source VLANs cannot be monitored.  You can set a combined maximum of four local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions.

name: show monitor
detail: Display information about all Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) and Remote SPAN (RSPAN) sessions.
syntax: show monitor [session {session_number | all | local | range list | remote} [detail]]

params.name: session
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about specified SPAN sessions.

params.name: session_number
params.description: The session number identified with the SPAN or RSPAN session. The range is 1 to 68. However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, you are limited to a combined maximum of two local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions, and the range is 1 to 66.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all SPAN sessions.

params.name: local
params.description: (Optional) Displays only local SPAN sessions.

params.name: range list
params.description: (Optional) Displays a range of SPAN sessions, where list is the range of valid sessions. The range is either a single session or a range of sessions described by two numbers, the lower one first, separated by a hyphen. Do not enter any spaces between comma-separated parameters or in hyphen-specified ranges.

params.name: Note
params.description: This keyword is available only in privileged EXEC mode.

params.name: remote
params.description: (Optional) Displays only remote SPAN sessions.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about the specified sessions.

usageSummary: The output is the same for the show monitor command and the show monitor session all command.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15. 2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 313 Network Management show monitor Maximum number of SPAN source sessions: 4 (applies to source and local sessions) However if this switch is stacked with Catalyst 2960-S switches, you are limited to a combined maximum of two local SPAN sessions and RSPAN source sessions.

name: snmp-server enable traps
detail: Enable the device to send Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) notifications for various traps or inform requests to the network management system (NMS).
syntax: snmp-server enable traps [bridge | cluster | config | copy-config | cpu threshold | entity | envmon | errdisable | flash | fru-ctrl | hsrp | ipmulticast | mac-notification | msdp | ospf | pim | port-security | rtr | snmp | storm-control | stpx | syslog | tty | vlan-membership | vlancreate | vlandelete | vtp ] entity | envmon | errdisable | flash | fru-ctrl | hsrp | ipmulticast | mac-notification | msdp | ospf | pim | port-security | rtr | snmp | storm-control | stpx | syslog | tty | vlan-membership | vlancreate | vlandelete | vtp ]
noForm: no snmp-server enable traps [bridge | cluster | config | copy-config | cpu threshold |

params.name: bridge
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP STP Bridge MIB traps.*

params.name: cluster
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP cluster traps.

params.name: config
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP configuration traps.

params.name: copy-config
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP copy-configuration traps.

params.name: cpu threshold
params.description: (Optional) Enables CPU related traps.*

params.name: entity
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP entity traps.

params.name: envmon
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP environmental monitor traps.*

params.name: errdisable
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP errdisable notification traps.*

params.name: flash
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP FLASH notification traps.*

params.name: fru-ctrl
params.description: (Optional) Generates entity field-replaceable unit (FRU) control traps. In a device stack, this trap refers to the insertion or removal of a device in the stack.

params.name: hsrp
params.description: (Optional) Enables Hot Standby Router Protocol (HSRP) traps.

params.name: ipmulticast
params.description: (Optional) Enables IP multicast routing traps.

params.name: mac-notification
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP MAC Notification traps.*

params.name: msdp
params.description: (Optional) Enables Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) traps.

params.name: ospf
params.description: (Optional) Enables Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) traps.

params.name: pim
params.description: (Optional) Enables Protocol-Independent Multicast (PIM) traps.

params.name: port-security
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP port security traps.*

params.name: rtr
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP Response Time Reporter (RTR) traps. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 316 Network Management snmp-server enable traps

params.name: snmp
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP traps.*

params.name: storm-control
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP storm-control trap parameters.*

params.name: stpx
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP STPX MIB traps.*

params.name: syslog
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP syslog traps.

params.name: tty
params.description: (Optional) Sends TCP connection traps. This is enabled by default.

params.name: vlan-membership
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP VLAN membership traps.

params.name: vlancreate
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP VLAN-created traps.

params.name: vlandelete
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP VLAN-deleted traps.

params.name: vtp
params.description: (Optional) Enables VLAN Trunking Protocol (VTP) traps.

usageSummary: The command options marked with an asterisk in the table above have subcommands.  For more information on these subcommands, see the Related Commands section below.  Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.

name: snmp-server enable traps bridge

params.name: newroot
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP STP bridge MIB new root traps. topologychange (Optional) Enables SNMP STP bridge MIB topology change traps.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps cpu

params.name: threshold
params.description: (Optional) Enables CPU threshold notification.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps envmon

params.name: fan
params.description: (Optional) Enables fan traps.

params.name: shutdown
params.description: (Optional) Enables environmental monitor shutdown traps.

params.name: status
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP environmental status-change traps.

params.name: supply
params.description: (Optional) Enables environmental monitor power-supply traps. temperature (Optional) Enables environmental monitor temperature traps.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps errdisable
noForm: no snmp-server enable traps errdisable [notification-rate number-of-notifications]

params.name: notification-rate
params.description: (Optional) Specifies number of notifications per minute as the

params.name: number-of-notifications
params.description: notification rate. Accepted values are from 0 to 10000.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps flash
usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps mac-notification

params.name: change
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP MAC change traps.

params.name: move
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP MAC move traps. threshold (Optional) Enables SNMP MAC threshold traps.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps port-security
noForm: no snmp-server enable traps port-security [trap-rate value]

params.name: trap-rate
params.description: (Optional) Sets the maximum number of port-security traps sent per second. The range is

params.name: value
params.description: from 0 to 1000; the default is 0 (no limit imposed; a trap is sent at every occurrence).

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps rtr
detail: Enable the sending of Cisco IOS IP Service Level Agreements (SLAs) Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap notifications.
usageSummary: This command controls (enables or disables) Cisco IOS IP SLAs notifications, as defined in the Response Time Monitor MIB (CISCO-RTTMON-MIB).  The snmp-server enable traps rtrcommand is used in conjunction with the snmp-server hostcommand.  Use the snmp-server host command to specify which host or hosts receive SNMP notifications.

name: snmp-server enable traps snmp

params.name: coldstart
params.description: (Optional) Enables cold start traps.

params.name: linkdown
params.description: (Optional) Enables linkdown traps.

params.name: linkup
params.description: (Optional) Enables linkup traps.

params.name: warmstart
params.description: (Optional) Enables warmstart traps.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps storm-control

params.name: trap-rate
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the SNMP storm-control trap rate in minutes. Accepted

params.name: number-of-minutes
params.description: values are from 0 to 1000.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.

name: snmp-server enable traps stpx

params.name: inconsistency
params.description: (Optional) Enables SNMP STPX MIB inconsistency update traps. loop-inconsistency (Optional) Enables SNMP STPX MIB loop inconsistency update traps. root-inconsistency (Optional) Enables SNMP STPX MIB root inconsistency update traps.

usageSummary: Specify the host (NMS) that receives the traps by using the snmp-server host global configuration command.  If no trap types are specified, all trap types are sent.
